channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: How ions squeeze water out of n-type ladder organic mixed conductors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02490-0

Unlike common organic mixed ionic–electronic conductors that swell upon electrochemical doping, poly(benzimidazobenzophenanthroline) exhibits an unexpected cation-dependent deswelling at high doping levels. Operando measurements and modelling show that protic cations drive this response by expelling water and localizing charge through strong interactions with the polymer backbone, revealing a distinct deswelling mechanism.
item.dc:title: How ions squeeze water out of n-type ladder organic mixed conductors

item.title: Topological control of spontaneous failure in active nematic solids
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02493-x

Topological defect quadrupoles orchestrate predictable failure patterns in active nematic solid networks.
item.dc:title: Topological control of spontaneous failure in active nematic solids
item.dc:creator: Sheng Chen
Matthew Ricci
A. Pasha Tabatabai
Zachary Gao Sun
Sven Witthaus
Suraj Shankar
Mor Nitzan
Michael P. Murrell

item.title: An ångström-scale Janus aperture as a gas flow rectifier
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02513-w

Gas transport through an ångström-sized Janus aperture in graphene, which is created by ozone etching, is investigated by using high-resolution transport measurements and high-throughput atomistic simulations. Rectified gas flow for various gases is demonstrated with rectification ratios of up to two orders of magnitude for oxygen.
item.dc:title: An ångström-scale Janus aperture as a gas flow rectifier
item.dc:creator: Hongwei Duan
Jing Yang
Nianjie Liang
Xiaobo Chen
Shengping Zhang
Anshul Saxena
Zeyu Zhuang
Ruiyang Song
Junhe Tong
Kaihui Liu
Narayana R. Aluru
Bai Song
Luda Wang

item.title: Altermagnetic multiferroics with symmetry-locked magnetoelectric coupling
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02518-5

We discuss altermagnetic multiferroics, materials hosting distinct advantages for low-power spintronic devices, including a zero net magnetization that eliminates stray fields, a momentum-dependent spin splitting enabling controllable spin currents and an intrinsic strong magnetoelectric coupling originating from the spin space symmetry.
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic multiferroics with symmetry-locked magnetoelectric coupling
item.dc:creator: Wei Sun
Changhong Yang
Xiaotian Wang
Shifeng Huang
Zhenxiang Cheng

item.title: Six-state clock physics in an atomically thin antiferromagnet
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02516-7

Nonlinear optical micropolarimetry and atomistic Monte Carlo simulations of monolayer NiPS3 evidence a Berezinskii–Kosterlitz–Thouless phase that, with decreasing temperature, gives way to long-range order consistent with a six-state clock model.
item.dc:title: Six-state clock physics in an atomically thin antiferromagnet
item.dc:creator: Frank Y. Gao
Dong Seob Kim
Chao Lei
Ajesh Kumar
Xinyue Peng
Xiaohui Liu
Francesco Barantani
Shangjie Zhang
Kyoung Pyo Lee
Kalaivanan Raju
David Lujan
Saba Arash
Sankar Raman
Shang-Fan Lee
Mengxing Ye
Xiaoqin Li
Allan H. MacDonald
Edoardo Baldini

item.title: Crystal-facet-directed all-vacuum-deposited perovskite solar cells
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02494-w

The vacuum process is scalable and solvent free, yet all-vacuum-deposited perovskite solar cells still trail solution-processed counterparts. Facet-directed co-evaporation yields (100)-oriented mixed-halide wide-bandgap films for efficient, stable single-junction cells and perovskite–silicon tandem cells.
item.dc:title: Crystal-facet-directed all-vacuum-deposited perovskite solar cells
item.dc:creator: Xinyi Shen
Wing Tung Hui
Shuaifeng Hu
Fengning Yang
Junke Wang

item.title: Interface complexes unlock ultrahigh-strength martensite
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02497-7

A martensitic medium-entropy alloy surpasses the long-standing strength–ductility ceiling of conventional ultrahigh-strength steels. The strengthening without sacrificing ductility originates from Mo–B–C interface complexes that stabilize small-angle grain boundaries.
item.dc:title: Interface complexes unlock ultrahigh-strength martensite
item.dc:creator: Min Seok Kim
Eun Soo Park

item.title: Ultralow contact resistance in halide perovskite devices
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02482-0

A charge transfer between metal oxide and halide perovskites occurring beneath the metal contact surface locally dopes the semiconductor, dramatically reducing contact resistance and enabling efficient low-voltage charge injection.
item.dc:title: Ultralow contact resistance in halide perovskite devices
item.dc:creator: Taoyu Zou
Yong-Young Noh
